articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Substitution, FC Bayern München. Leon Goretzka replaces Raphaël Guerreiro because of an injury.
Eric Dier (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Waldemar Anton (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.

Goal!  VfB Stuttgart 1, FC Bayern München 0. Leonidas Stergiou (VfB Stuttgart) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Deniz Undav with a through ball.
Goal!  VfB Stuttgart 1, FC Bayern München 1. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom right corner.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Dayot Upamecano replaces Eric Dier because of an injury.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Bryan Zaragoza replaces Serge Gnabry.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Noussair Mazraoui replaces Joshua Kimmich.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Leroy Sané replaces Mathys Tel.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Mahmoud Dahoud replaces Anthony Rouault.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Jeong Woo-Yeong replaces Chris Führich.
Aleksandar Pavlovic (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Silas Katompa Mvumpa replaces Jamie Leweling.
Goal!  VfB Stuttgart 2, FC Bayern München 1. Jeong Woo-Yeong (VfB Stuttgart) header from the right side of the six yard box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Silas Katompa Mvumpa with a cross.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Luca Raimund replaces Deniz Undav.
Goal!  VfB Stuttgart 3, FC Bayern München 1. Silas Katompa Mvumpa (VfB Stuttgart) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom right corner.

article.headline: Bayern slump to loss at Stuttgart as Madrid await
article.description: VfB Stuttgart scored two late goals to snatch a 3-1 win over visitors Bayern Munich in the Bundesliga on Saturday, spoiling the Bavarians' preparations for their Champions League semi-final return leg against Real Madrid next week.

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Highlight (VfB Stuttgart vs. Bayern Munich - Game Highlights) 2024/05/04 ESHEET

article.story:
Stuttgart substitutes Jeong Woo-yeong and Silas scored late goals to snatch a 3-1 win over visitors Bayern Munich in the Bundesliga on Saturday, spoiling the Bavarians' preparations for their Champions League semifinal return leg against Real Madrid.

Bayern, who are in second place on 69 points, two ahead of Stuttgart in third with two games left, face Madrid in Spain on Wednesday after their 2-2 draw in Munich.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

The defeat added to an already disappointing domestic campaign for Thomas Tuchel's team, with the coach set to leave at the end of the season. It also comes after the club have been turned down by a number of coaches to succeed Tuchel, including Ralf Rangnick, turned down offers from the Bavarians.

For Stuttgart, however, the result crowned what has been an exceptional campaign with a club-record equalling 21st win of the season.

The hosts took a deserved lead through Leonidas Stergiou's flick over goalkeeper Manuel Neuer just before the half hour mark after missing three golden chances.

Much-changed Bayern levelled with a penalty from Harry Kane in the 37th minute, his 36th league goal of the season.

Kane is now five goals behind the all-time Bundesliga scoring record for a season of 41 goals set by Robert Lewandowski in 2021.

It was Suttgart who looked sharper, however, and substitute Jeong Woo-yeong beat Bayern goalkeeper Manuel Neuer at the far post in the 84th minute to put the hosts back in front.

The 25-year-old Silas sealed the win with a low drive in stoppage time after a Bayern defensive error.
